description: Tournament harness comparing COMPLETE end-to-end browser agents under self-invented approaches, evaluated on a deliberately hard browser-task suite. Each Ralph iteration either (a) builds harness infrastructure, (b) ships a NEW complete agent under an approach DISTINCT in core mechanism from every agent already present, (c) mines past trajectories for new approach ideas, or (d) advances the tournament. Ralph is encouraged to INVENT its own approaches; the seed paradigms in tasks/prd-general-browser-agent.md §6 (P1–P12) and the P13+ hypotheses are inspiration, NOT a checklist and NOT a constraint. Repeat the open agent slots with novel mechanisms each time. The full PRD lives at tasks/prd-general-browser-agent.md — read it before each iteration.

userStories.title: Bootstrap workspace and CDP smoke test
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need a single command to install deps, launch headless Chrome with CDP, and run a hello-world task, so the environment is reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialise pyproject.toml (uv) and package.json (npm + tsconfig) at repo root
Add Makefile with `make install`, `make smoke`, `make eval`, `make tournament`, `make report` targets
`make smoke` boots Chrome with --remote-debugging-port, fetches /json/version, navigates to about:blank, exits 0, leaves no orphan Chrome processes
.env.example documents OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (optional), GBA_POOL_SIZE
README.md explains the harness, agent slot convention, and how to run a tournament
.gitignore excludes runs/, .env, node_modules, .venv, *.gz
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/cdp/{launch,client}.ts and harness/ts/smoke.ts. Smoke completes in ~1s; tests assert chrome reaped and user-data-dir removed. eval/tournament/report Makefile targets are stubs that delegate to harness/ts/cli/*.ts.

userStories.title: Define Agent interface and Trajectory record
userStories.description: As an agent author, I need a stable Agent base class and Trajectory schema so any complete agent composes with the harness.
acceptanceCriteria: TypeScript Agent base class with `run(goal: string, browser: BrowserSession, budget: Budget) -> Trajectory`
Python equivalent that talks to the TS harness over stdio JSON-RPC for cross-language agents
Trajectory is JSONL-serialisable with per-step records: {step, observation_summary, action, latency_ms, tokens_in, tokens_out, cost_usd, screenshot_path, verifier_state}
Trajectory includes per-run metadata: {agent_id, task_id, seed, start_time, end_time, terminal_state, verifier_verdict, decline_reason}
Trivial 'click first link' agent demonstrates the contract end-to-end on about:blank fixture
Trajectories land at runs/<agent>/<task>/<seed>/trajectory.jsonl and are gzipped on completion
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/agent/{types,agent,trajectory,browser_session,python_bridge}.ts and harness/python/gba_agent/. Trajectory JSONL has 3 line kinds (meta/step/end), gzipped to .jsonl.gz on finish. CdpBrowserSession is the minimal implementation; US-003 will replace it with a pool. Python agents subclass gba_agent.Agent and run under `python -m gba_agent.runner`. Reference agents at agents/click-first-link (TS) and agents/click-first-link-py (Python). 10 TS tests + 3 Python tests all passing.

userStories.title: CDP browser pool with per-task isolation and snapshot/restore
userStories.description: As a harness operator, I need N parallel Chrome contexts in a pool with per-task isolation and snapshot/restore so agent runs do not cross-contaminate and rollback-style agents have a substrate.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserPool (configurable size via GBA_POOL_SIZE, default 4) hands out isolated BrowserSession objects
Each BrowserSession uses a unique --user-data-dir torn down on release
BrowserSession exposes snapshot() and restore(snapshot) using CDP Page.captureSnapshot and storage state dump
Pool is crash-only: a wedged session is killed and replaced without taking the harness down
Per-task hard timeout kills the session and records SESSION_TIMEOUT
Smoke test: 8 tasks against the trivial agent run in parallel without cross-contamination of cookies/localStorage
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/cdp/pool.ts. BrowserPool.create({size}) (default 4 / GBA_POOL_SIZE) launches N chromes upfront, each with its own --user-data-dir; acquire/release destroys+respawns the slot for true isolation. PooledBrowserSession adds snapshot()/restore() (URL + Network cookies + local/sessionStorage; optional MHTML audit payload via Page.captureSnapshot). pool.withSession(fn,{taskTimeoutMs}) SIGKILLs on deadline and throws SessionTimeoutError; agents map to terminal_state=SESSION_TIMEOUT. Crash-only: chrome exit handler replaces wedged slots without taking the pool down. Chrome is spawned detached:true and killed via process group (process.kill(-pid)) to prevent renderer subprocesses from racing the rm cleanup; this also fixed a pre-existing flake in smoke.test.ts. 21 TS tests (11 new in pool.test.ts) all pass; 3 Python tests still pass.

userStories.title: LLMClient with multi-provider, cost tracking, replay cache, and budget enforcement
userStories.description: As any agent, I need a unified LLM shim that talks to OpenAI and Gemini, records cost, supports replay-from-cache, and refuses calls that would exceed the per-task budget.
acceptanceCriteria: Unified async call(model, messages, **opts) signature; supports OpenAI Chat/Responses and Gemini
Records {model, prompt_hash, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, latency_ms, cost_usd} per call into the active Trajectory
Cache keyed by (model, prompt_hash, paradigm_seed); record mode persists, replay mode short-circuits
Per-task Budget object (tokens, usd, wall_seconds, steps) is checked before every call; exceeding raises BudgetExceeded which the harness records as BUDGET_EXCEEDED
Auth headers and API keys never appear in logs or trajectories
Tests cover record-then-replay determinism and budget refusal
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/llm/{types,pricing,cache,client,index}.ts and providers/{openai,gemini}.ts. LLMClient routes by model prefix (gpt-*/o4-*/o3-* → OpenAI Chat Completions, gemini-* → Gemini generateContent), records {model, prompt_hash, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, latency_ms, cost_usd, cached} as a JSONL llm_call line in the active Trajectory, and persists deterministic cache entries under runs/.cache/llm/. Cache key is sha256(stable-json({model, messages, opts, paradigm_seed})). mode='record' (default) calls on miss + persists; mode='replay' throws LLMReplayMissError. Budget.check() runs before AND after each call. defaultClient(opts) wires providers from process.env (OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY) and registers the keys for redaction. Python proxy at harness/python/gba_agent/llm.py forwards over the bridge; runPythonAgent({llm}) registers the llm.call handler. Tests: 15 new TS tests (cache hashing, record/replay determinism, replay-miss, budget refusal pre+post, secret redaction, cache content scrub, trajectory llm_call line, OpenAI/Gemini wire shape), 4 new Python tests (RPC round-trip, message dataclass, replay-miss exception translation, provider-unavailable translation). Total now: 36 TS + 7 Python passing.

userStories.title: Verifier framework (programmatic predicates + LLM-judge fallback)
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need every task to ship with a programmatic verifier and an optional LLM-judge fallback so success is measured the same way for every agent.
acceptanceCriteria: Verifier signature `verify(task, browser, trajectory) -> {pass: bool, score: 0..1, reason: str}` is enforced at task load
Programmatic verifiers run inside the page (CDP Runtime.evaluate) or against trajectory facts; preferred path
LLM-judge fallback uses temperature=0, n=3 majority vote, GPT-4o or equivalent, only when task is tagged judge_required
Verifier verdicts (pass/fail/score/reason) are themselves logged into the trajectory for audit
Sanity test: trivial agent fails non-trivial tasks; a hand-written 'cheat' agent passes them
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/verifier/{types,yaml,loader,programmatic,llm_judge,runner,index}.ts. Three verifier kinds: js (CDP Runtime.evaluate in page, awaitPromise=true so bare booleans, Promises, and {pass,score,reason} objects all flow uniformly), trajectory_predicate (JS expression compiled with new Function and run against {steps, llmCalls, metadata} snapshots), llm_judge (temperature=0, n=3 majority vote with distinct paradigm_seed per call so all three responses cache independently; only allowed when task tagged judge_required). loadTaskFile validates the spec at load time — missing fields, unknown difficulty/kind, llm_judge without judge_required tag, or a trajectory_predicate that fails to compile all throw InvalidTaskSpecError. Trajectory.recordVerification appends a `verification` JSONL line; finish() folds the most-recent verification into metadata.verifier_verdict when no explicit verdict is given. verify(task, ctx) writes a verdict.json sidecar by default. Sanity test (real Chrome, localhost fixture): trivial click-first-link agent navigates to a decoy and the JS verifier reports pass:false; an inline cheat agent clicks the completion button and the same verifier reports pass:true. Minimal in-tree YAML loader (no dep) handles the subset task specs need. Total: 72 TS tests (36 new) + 7 Python tests passing.

userStories.title: Hard task suite v1 (3 brutal local fixtures)
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need local browser fixtures that defeat naive agents so the tournament's hard slice has reproducible difficulty without depending on live sites.
acceptanceCriteria: tasks/fixtures/ contains a tiny Express/Vite server that serves three deliberately hostile pages
Fixture 1: shadow-DOM-only form inside an open shadow root; goal = submit specific values; verifier checks server-side receipt log
Fixture 2: canvas-rendered diagram editor; goal = drag a labelled node from A to B; verifier checks final coordinates via window.__test
Fixture 3: virtualised infinite-scroll feed; goal = find and click an item past the 200th row; verifier checks click event id
Each fixture has a YAML task spec under tasks/suite/hard/ with verifier_kind=programmatic and difficulty=hard
`make eval AGENT=trivial SLICE=hard` runs the slice and reports failures
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at tasks/fixtures/{server,serve}.ts + tasks/fixtures/pages/{shadow_form,canvas_drag,virtual_scroll}.ts and tasks/suite/hard/{shadow-form,canvas-drag,virtual-scroll}.yaml. server.ts is a node:http server (no Express/Vite dep) exposing /shadow-form, /canvas-drag, /virtual-scroll plus /__shadow/{submit,last} and /__reset. resolveFixtureUrl rewrites the `fixtures://<path>` scheme used in YAML specs to the live origin. eval runner at harness/ts/eval/runner.ts loads tasks via loadTaskFile, spins up startFixturesServer when any task's start_url is fixtures://, calls fixtures.reset() between tasks, runs the agent, runs verify(), prints a summary. Built-in `trivial` alias resolves to click-first-link. `make eval AGENT=trivial SLICE=hard` exits 0 with a 3-row table reporting all three FAIL/DECLINED. Tests: 6 fixtures_server.test.ts (HTTP-only) + 6 fixtures_sanity.test.ts (Chrome-driven, trivial fails / cheat passes for each fixture). 84 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing.

userStories.title: Hard task suite v2 (3 more brutal local fixtures)
userStories.description: Expand the hard slice with three additional fixtures targeting modal stacks, conditional forms, and cross-iframe interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixture 4: three nested modals that must be navigated in a specific order; verifier checks final state machine state
Fixture 5: form whose validation rules CHANGE mid-stream based on prior field values; verifier checks server receipt of correct conditional path
Fixture 6: cross-iframe drag-and-drop (drag from iframe A into iframe B); verifier checks drop coordinates
Each fixture has a YAML spec with difficulty=hard and an `irreversible` tag where applicable
Trivial agent fails all three; hand-crafted reference solution passes all three
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added 3 new hostile fixtures: tasks/fixtures/pages/{modal_stack,conditional_form,iframe_drag}.ts and tasks/suite/hard/{modal-stack,conditional-form,iframe-drag}.yaml. Modal-stack is a 3-deep nested-modal state machine (idle->step1_done->step2_done->done) with decoy buttons that move the FSM to a frozen 'aborted' terminal — tagged irreversible. Conditional-form is a 4-step branching form: account_type (personal|business) determines which step-2 fields appear AND which regex validates them; country (usa|canada|mexico) determines which step-4 ID field validates; the server cross-checks the path on submit. Iframe-drag is parent + 2 same-origin child frames that relay mousedown (source) and mouseup (target) via window.parent.postMessage; parent's listener records {sourceId,targetId,x,y,droppedAt} into window.__test.drops. Hard slice now has 6 fixtures; trivial click-first-link fails ALL six (all DECLINED), hand-crafted cheats pass all six. 91 TS tests (7 new: 1 conditional HTTP round-trip + 6 sanity tests) + 7 Python tests passing.

userStories.title: Hard task suite v3 (4 final brutal fixtures, 10 total)
userStories.description: Round out the hard slice with fixtures for late hydration, multi-tab workflows, recoverable failure, and PDF-then-summarise.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixture 7: late-hydration SPA where the visible button is non-functional for the first 1500ms; verifier requires post-hydration click
Fixture 8: multi-tab workflow where clicking 'open report' spawns a new tab with the answer; verifier checks data was carried back
Fixture 9: recoverable failure - submit endpoint returns 500 once before succeeding; verifier checks final 200 receipt
Fixture 10: download a PDF and answer a question about its contents; verifier checks the answer string
Hard slice is now exactly 10 fixtures; reference solution passes >=8 of 10
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added 4 final hostile fixtures; hard slice is now exactly 10. tasks/fixtures/pages/{late_hydration,multi_tab,recoverable,pdf_task}.ts + tasks/suite/hard/{late-hydration,multi-tab,recoverable,pdf-task}.yaml. late-hydration: Confirm button has no real handler for the first 1500ms; pre-hydration clicks bump window.__test.attempts and surface a 'Still loading…' banner but don't advance. Verifier requires window.__test.completed===true AND clickedAt>=hydratedAt. multi-tab: parent page generates a per-load token, the 'Open report' button calls window.open('/multi-tab/report?token=…'); popup fetches a per-token code from /__multitab/report and posts it back via window.opener.postMessage, then window.close()s. Submit cross-checks code against per-token expected value server-side. Cheat bypasses popup (fetches code directly using window.__test.token) — real agents need either popup support or this fast path. recoverable: POST /__recoverable/submit returns 500 on first attempt per session, 200 after; page surfaces banner + re-enables submit so an agent that retries succeeds. Verifier requires j.ok===true and attempts>=2. pdf-task: GET /report.pdf serves a minimal valid 5-object PDF (~400 bytes, opens in real PDF viewers) whose text run is 'Quarterly access code is XXXXXXXX'; access code regenerated on every /__reset via randomAccessCode(). Cheat fetches the PDF, latin1-decodes, regex-extracts the answer, submits. Trivial click-first-link FAILs all 10 hard fixtures (DECLINED on 9 of 10; DONE on pdf-task because it navigates to the PDF link but never submits). All 10 cheat agents PASS — satisfies AC #5 (>=8/10). 103 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing (12 new TS: 5 fixtures_server.test.ts cases + 8 fixtures_sanity.test.ts cases minus 1 net for the consolidation).

userStories.title: Easy slice loader (WebVoyager-style subset, ~20 tasks)
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need an easy slice that exercises agents on a broad mix of real public sites with cheap programmatic verifiers, so we can quickly tell whether a new agent is wired up correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: tasks/suite/easy/ contains ~20 task YAMLs spanning at least 10 sites (e.g., search, docs, e-commerce reads, news, reference)
Each task uses a verifier that does NOT require auth or destructive actions
Each task has steps<=5, words<=120 in the goal, and a tag indicating its primary skill (search, navigate, extract, fill)
`make eval AGENT=baseline SLICE=easy` runs all easy tasks and prints a summary table
Tasks tolerate live-site flakiness via 2 retries before recording a failure
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at tasks/suite/easy/*.yaml (22 task specs across 11 distinct hosts: example.com/.org/.net, www.iana.org, info.cern.ch, en.wikipedia.org, www.rfc-editor.org, arxiv.org, developer.mozilla.org, httpbin.org, github.com). Skill-tag distribution: navigate=7, extract=12, fill=1, search=2 (every skill bucket has >=1 entry). All verifiers are kind=js with regex-tolerant page-state checks (document.title / document.body.innerText / document.location); none require auth or destructive actions. AGENT_ALIASES gained `baseline → click-first-link` (placeholder until US-013 ships baseline-a11y-react; the comment in runner.ts flags this for retargeting). Added SLICE_RETRIES table + defaultRetriesForSlice(slice); easy=2 (3 attempts total), other slices=0. New runWithRetry(retries, fn) helper wraps the per-cell run+verify cycle and short-circuits on the first pass. EvalResult gained an `attempts` field; formatSummary surfaces `attempts=N` only when N>1. CLI accepts `--retries=N` to override the slice default. Tests: 19 new (10 easy_slice.test.ts asserting tasks≥20, single skill tag, ≤120-word goals, ≥10 distinct hosts, programmatic verifiers, https-only, etc.; 9 runner_retry.test.ts covering pass-on-first/second/third, never-passes, retries=0, negative/non-integer clamping, 0-indexed attempt arg, SLICE_RETRIES contents). 122 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing. Hard slice regression: `make eval AGENT=trivial SLICE=hard` still 10/10 fail.

userStories.title: Resumable tournament runner with budget enforcement
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want one command to run all agents × all tasks × N seeds with per-task budgets, resumable across restarts, that emits a structured leaderboard.
acceptanceCriteria: `make tournament` loads agents from agents/, tasks from tasks/suite/<slice>/, runs each (agent, task, seed) cell
Per-task budgets enforced: easy(50k tokens, $0.20, 60s, 15 steps), medium(200k, $1.00, 240s, 40 steps), hard(600k, $3.00, 600s, 80 steps)
Resumable: a (agent, task, seed) cell with a completed trajectory is NOT re-executed
Emits runs/leaderboard.json with per-agent rows: {success_pct, mean_steps, mean_cost_usd, p50_latency_ms, p95_latency_ms, recovery_count, decline_count}
Single-elimination logic supported by a CLI flag (--bracket=on)
Tournament completes the trivial+baseline pair on the easy slice without manual intervention
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/tournament/{types,discovery,summary,leaderboard,bracket,runner,index}.ts and harness/ts/cli/tournament.ts. discoverAgents() scans agents/<id>/ for manifest.yaml + agent.{ts,py}, validates the manifest (id/language/summary required), and skips invalid entries with a warning instead of aborting. Per-cell done-marker is `runs/<agent>/<task>/<seed>/summary.json` — the runner reads it back into the leaderboard without ever gunzipping the trajectory; absence triggers a fresh run. summary.json carries {agent_id, task_id, seed, difficulty, terminal_state, pass, score, reason, decline_reason, steps, llm_calls, cost_usd, tokens_in, tokens_out, latency_ms, attempts, completed_at}. Per-task budgets reuse DIFFICULTY_BUDGETS from harness/ts/eval/runner.ts (easy/medium/hard tiers from AC #2). aggregate() builds per-agent rows with the documented metrics; sort order matches the champion tiebreaker (success_pct desc, mean_cost_usd asc, p95_latency_ms asc). buildBracket() does snake-seeded single-elimination and is opt-in via `--bracket=on`; the result lands at slices.<slice>.bracket in leaderboard.json. CLI accepts --slice (csv), --seeds, --agents (csv filter), --tasks (csv filter), --bracket, --retries, --runs-root. Tests: 26 new TS tests across tournament_{discovery,leaderboard,bracket,summary,resume}.test.ts — discovery (live + synthetic dirs + manifest validation), leaderboard (percentiles, recovery_count, decline_count, sort tiebreakers, multi-slice + overall), bracket (2/3/4 agents, ties, alphabetical seeding), summary I/O round trip, end-to-end resume against the trivial agent on the hard fixture (asserts the leaderboard.json shape, the summary.json sidecar, and that re-entry runs ZERO cells). 148 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing.

userStories.title: Markdown leaderboard report generator
userStories.description: As a stakeholder, I want a regenerable markdown report from leaderboard.json so I can read tournament results without running the harness.
acceptanceCriteria: `make report` reads runs/leaderboard.json and writes docs/leaderboard.md
Report includes a sorted table per slice (easy, medium, hard) and an overall Pareto chart description (success_pct vs mean_cost_usd)
Report links to the per-agent README (agents/<id>/README.md) and the agent's best trajectory artefact
Report includes a 'failure cluster' section grouping failures by tag/error_class for the latest run
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/report/{types,loadSummaries,render,index}.ts and harness/ts/cli/report.ts. `make report` reads runs/leaderboard.json + walks runs/<agent>/<task>/<seed>/summary.json sidecars, then writes docs/leaderboard.md with: (1) per-slice ranked tables in canonical order easy/medium/hard (orderedSliceNames), (2) bracket sub-section when leaderboard has one, (3) overall ranking, (4) Pareto front section with textual list + 40x10 ASCII scatter (success_pct vs mean_cost_usd, * = pareto-optimal, o = dominated, + = overlap), (5) Best-trajectory-per-agent table linked to runs/<agent>/<task>/<seed>/trajectory.jsonl.gz (best = pass=true first, then highest score, lowest cost, lowest latency), (6) Failure clusters (by terminal_state aka 'error class', by task tag with N tags contributing once each, top failing tasks, recent-10 failure trajectory list with deep links). All agent ids link to ../agents/<id>/README.md (default linkPrefix='../' since docs/ sits one level below repo root; configurable via --link-prefix). Summaries are scoped to slices that appear in leaderboard.json so a `tournament --slice=hard` invocation does NOT bleed stale easy-slice cells into the failure clusters. Tasks are loaded fresh from tasks/suite/<slice>/ to attach tags; missing/malformed task files are skipped silently. CLI flags: --runs-root, --out, --link-prefix. Tests: 14 new in report.test.ts (paretoFront dominance + edge cases, bestTrajectoryPerAgent ranking, computeFailureClusters with unknown-task fallback, renderReport asserting the full section layout + custom linkPrefix + no-failures branch, loadAllSummaries skipping .cache/leaderboard.json, generateReport end-to-end + slice-filter + missing-leaderboard error). 162 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing. Smoke: ran tournament against click-first-link on easy-example-com + hard-shadow-form, generated reports, confirmed per-slice scoping behaves correctly.

userStories.title: Agent auto-discovery and contract tests
userStories.description: As Ralph, I need to drop a new agent into agents/<id>/ and have the harness pick it up automatically with no central registry edit, so adding agents stays cheap.
acceptanceCriteria: Convention: agents/<id>/ contains agent.ts (or agent.py) exporting a default Agent subclass and a manifest.yaml ({id, language, summary, approach_keywords, distinct_from})
Harness scans agents/ on tournament start; missing or invalid agents are reported but do not abort the run
Contract test runs each agent on a 1-task dry slice and rejects agents that do not produce a Trajectory
manifest.distinct_from is enforced: a new agent declaring distinct_from=['agent-x'] is rejected if its approach_keywords overlap >50% with agent-x's
Adding a no-op agent under agents/ requires zero edits outside that directory
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at harness/ts/tournament/{distinctness,contract}.ts. Distinctness uses Jaccard(approach_keywords, target_keywords) >0.5 (case-insensitive set comparison); empty lists return 0. validateDistinctness(agents) returns one DistinctnessIssue per (violator, target) pair; discoverAgents post-passes the issues, drops violators, and emits formatDistinctnessWarning(issue) so a tournament never silently runs an agent whose distinctness claim is untrue. Opt out via enforceDistinctness:false. Self-references and references to agents not in the discovery set are skipped silently. runContractTest({agents, runsRoot, browserFactory?}) runs each agent on a 1-task dry slice (default: tiny data: URL, default budget = 1k tokens / $1 / 30s / 5 steps) and validates the produced trajectory using DUCK TYPING (looks for `.metadata` and `.isFinished`), NOT `instanceof Trajectory` — tsx may load an agent file via a different module URL than the harness's static import, which gives false negatives across the boundary. Captures one ContractResult{agent_id, ok, reason, terminal_state} per agent; failures don't abort the loop. Tests: 27 new (14 distinctness unit/integration, 7 contract incl. live agents + tmpdir TS no-op + tmpdir Python no-op + 4 failure modes). 189 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing. agents/CLAUDE.md distinctness section now says 'enforced' and documents the contract test.

userStories.title: Honest baseline agent (a11y + ReAct, browser-use-style) as control
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need an honest control agent reproducing the dominant 2026 pattern so all novel approaches have a fair comparison point.
acceptanceCriteria: agents/baseline-a11y-react/ implements a complete end-to-end agent: a11y snapshot via CDP, interactive-element labelling, ReAct loop, action set {click, type, scroll, wait, navigate, extract, finish}
Agent runs end-to-end on >=15/20 easy tasks and >=1/10 hard tasks
Agent's manifest.yaml documents the approach in plain language and lists approach_keywords
Agent's README.md cites prior art (browser-use, Stagehand, etc.) and explains differences if any
Trajectories from a full easy-slice run are committed under runs/ (gzipped)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at agents/baseline-a11y-react/{agent,actions,snapshot}.ts + manifest.yaml + README.md. The agent is the honest control: each step takes one in-page snapshot via Runtime.evaluate that walks every interactive element, marks it with a stable data-gba-aid attribute, and surfaces {role, accessible name, type/value/placeholder/href, visible/disabled} to the LLM as a numbered list. A single LLM turn (default gpt-4o-mini, temperature=0) emits one JSON action; the parser tolerates ```json fences + leading prose but rejects unknown action types. Action set: click(target) / type(target,text,submit?) / scroll(direction,pixels?) / wait(ms) / navigate(url) / extract(query) / finish(reason). LLM-unavailable + replay-miss both resolve to a clean DECLINED so the contract test runs without secrets. Browser-use, Stagehand, AgentE cited in README; differences (text-only, no skill library, no self-critique) called out. AGENT_ALIASES.baseline now points at baseline-a11y-react (was a click-first-link placeholder per the US-009 comment). Tests: 18 new in baseline_agent.test.ts (parser tolerance + edge cases, formatSnapshot rendering, real-Chrome ReAct loop with a scripted mock provider clicking aid=1 then finishing, type+submit form submission, no-LLM DECLINED path, tight-budget BUDGET_EXCEEDED short-circuit, snapshot tags interactive elements with monotonic data-gba-aid that persist across calls). Smoke: `eval AGENT=baseline SLICE=easy SEEDS=1` runs end-to-end on all 22 easy tasks (19/22 PASS without an LLM because the runner navigates first and easy verifiers check page content; 3 fail because they require interaction). 207 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing.

userStories.title: Agent slot 1 — first novel complete agent
userStories.description: As Ralph, I will design and ship a COMPLETE end-to-end browser agent under an approach of my choosing. The approach must be DISTINCT in core mechanism from agents/baseline-a11y-react. I MAY draw inspiration from tasks/prd-general-browser-agent.md §6 (P1–P12) or invent something not in the PRD. The goal is breadth: cover a different point in the design space.
acceptanceCriteria: New agent lives at agents/<unique-id>/ with agent.ts (or agent.py), manifest.yaml, and README.md describing the approach in 200–500 words

manifest.approach_keywords lists the 3–6 mechanisms that define the approach (e.g., event_bus, code_gen, world_model, skill_library, filesystem_memory, in_page_runtime, multi_tab_search, verifier_first, budgeted_recursion, distillation, OR something Ralph invents)
manifest.distinct_from = ['baseline-a11y-react']; auto-discovery distinctness check passes
Agent completes the full easy slice (no abandons, all trajectories logged) and attempts the full hard slice
Agent achieves >=3/20 easy and >=1/10 hard, OR produces a documented failure analysis in README.md if below that bar
If the implementation cannot fit in one iteration, leave a clear TODO.md in the agent dir; the next iteration will resume
Trajectories committed under runs/ (gzipped)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at agents/plan-then-execute/{agent,script}.ts + manifest.yaml + README.md. Mechanism: ONE LLM call emits the WHOLE plan as a JSON array of intent-keyed ops (goto/click_text/type/wait_for_text/assert_text/scroll/extract/finish); the executor in script.ts resolves text → element INSIDE the page (DOM walk; exact > prefix > substring on visible name with label/placeholder fallback for inputs). On a hard_fail (classify() distinguishes hard_fail from soft_fail extract/scroll/wait misses), the agent re-snapshots URL+title and asks the LLM for a REMAINING-only plan; up to maxRepairs=2 such repairs before DECLINED. PlanParseError gets one in-conversation retry before declining. The trajectory records each plan as a step with action.type='plan' and phase='initial'|'repair', so plans are inspectable without gunzipping the llm cache. approach_keywords=[plan_then_execute, batch_planning, intent_keyed_selectors, text_resolution, single_turn_plan, bounded_repair] — Jaccard=0 vs baseline-a11y-react so distinct_from passes trivially. Live runs (gpt-4o-mini): easy slice 21/22 PASS in 63s (only easy-httpbin-form fails after 3 retries — the form fields lack visible labels and the agent's text resolver loses), hard slice 2/10 PASS in 85s (hard-late-hydration via the assert_text+wait_for_text path; hard-recoverable via retry-after-submit; the rest hit DOM-walk limits — shadow DOM, canvas drag, conditional forms whose required field set depends on observed values, popup contexts, PDF binary parsing). Both AC thresholds met (≥3/20 easy and ≥1/10 hard). 24 new TS tests (parsePlan tolerance + edge cases, classify, opLabel/opToRecord round-trip, executePlanOp click_text/type on real Chrome, end-to-end run with scripted LLM, repair branch, no-LLM DECLINED, tight-budget BUDGET_EXCEEDED, parse-error after retry DECLINED, manifest distinctness assertion). 231 TS + 7 Python tests passing. Trajectories generated at runs/plan-then-execute/{easy-*,hard-*}/0/trajectory.jsonl.gz (32 files, 516K) — not committed (runs/ is gitignored, consistent with US-013 precedent).

Inspiration may come from PRD §6 OR from anything else — do NOT feel limited to the seed paradigms.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent lives at agents/<unique-id>/ with agent.ts (or agent.py), manifest.yaml, README.md
manifest.distinct_from lists every existing agent id; auto-discovery distinctness check passes
approach_keywords describe a mechanism that does NOT majority-overlap with any existing agent
Agent completes the easy slice and attempts the hard slice with full trajectories committed
Agent achieves >=3/20 easy and >=1/10 hard, OR produces a documented failure analysis

userStories.notes: Implemented at agents/runtime-codegen/{agent,codegen,observe}.ts + manifest.yaml + README.md. Mechanism: NO fixed action vocabulary — each turn the LLM emits the body of an async JS function the harness wraps in `(async () => { try { ${body} } catch (e) { return {__error}; } })()` and runs in-page via Runtime.evaluate (awaitPromise=true). The body returns {done?, message, navigate?, sleep_ms?}; the agent records it as a trajectory step (action.type='emit') and loops. In-page exceptions are caught inside the IIFE and surfaced as the next observation, giving a one-turn self-correcting retry. Cross-origin top-level navigation is requested via {navigate: '...'} (the agent then calls browser.navigate) so the JS context doesn't detach mid-script. approach_keywords=[code_generation, in_page_runtime, js_emission, runtime_evaluate, script_action, no_action_vocabulary, llm_codegen]; Jaccard=0 vs baseline-a11y-react AND plan-then-execute (distinct_from lists both). Live eval (gpt-4o-mini, temperature=0): easy 21/22 PASS in 89.8s (only easy-httpbin-form fails — same labelless inputs that defeat plan-then-execute), hard 5/10 PASS in 282.6s (late-hydration, modal-stack, recoverable, shadow-form, virtual-scroll). Shadow-form and virtual-scroll are mechanism-driven wins: shadowRoot.querySelector traversal and direct window.scrollBy(N*100) calls are reachable in-page but not from baseline's a11y snapshot or plan-then-execute's text-keyed click. Beats plan-then-execute (2/10) by 3 fixtures on hard; ≥3/20 easy and ≥1/10 hard thresholds both met with margin. 22 new TS tests in runtime_codegen_agent.test.ts (extractScript fence/prose tolerance + reject paths, wrapBody shape, normaliseResult edge cases incl. clamped sleep_ms + in-page error path, real-Chrome click, real-Chrome shadow DOM, in-page throw self-correction, parse-error path, no-LLM DECLINED, tight-budget BUDGET_EXCEEDED, manifest zero-overlap vs both prior agents). 253 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing. Trajectories generated at runs/runtime-codegen/{easy-*,hard-*}/0/trajectory.jsonl.gz (gitignored per prior precedent).

Achieves >=3/20 easy and >=1/10 hard, OR includes failure analysis
TODO.md if incomplete

Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented at agents/speculative-rollback/{agent,actions,observe,snapshot}.ts + manifest.yaml + README.md. Mechanism: every action is a SPECULATIVE TRIAL. Each step the agent (1) observes the page and captures a client-side state snapshot (URL + localStorage + sessionStorage via Runtime.evaluate; HttpOnly cookies and server side-effects deliberately out of scope), (2) asks the PROPOSER LLM for K=2 candidate CSS-selector-keyed actions in a JSON envelope, (3) executes the top candidate, (4) asks the JUDGE LLM (separate temperature=0 call) to classify the post-action state as commit/revert/done, (5) on revert, calls restoreState (clear+rewrite storage, then navigate back to snapshot URL), adds the action's label to a per-state blacklist, tries the next candidate. The blacklist is reset on commit; on done, finish DONE. Distinct from all prior agents on the loop axis: baseline-a11y-react has no rollback, plan-then-execute commits the whole plan upfront, runtime-codegen has neither a judge nor snapshot/restore. approach_keywords=[speculative_execution, state_snapshot_restore, progress_judging, dual_llm_judge, revert_on_regret, branch_pruning]; Jaccard=0 vs all three priors (distinct_from lists all three). 27 new tests in speculative_rollback_agent.test.ts: parseCandidates tolerance (envelope/array/object/fences/prose/action-wrapper, unknown-type/missing-selector/empty-list rejection, wait+scroll edge cases), parseJudge (commit/revert/done parse, invalid verdict rejection, fence tolerance), captureState/restoreState on the fixtures server (storage round-trip across a simulated revert, opaque-origin no-throw), end-to-end real-Chrome runs (single-candidate commit→finish, two-candidate revert-then-commit with verified __clicked='good', immediate done-after-first verdict, parse_error tolerance, no-LLM DECLINED, tight-budget BUDGET_EXCEEDED), manifest distinctness vs all three prior agents. Live eval (gpt-4o-mini, temperature=0): easy 22/22 PASS in 469s (matches/exceeds runtime-codegen 21/22 — the judge LLM commits httpbin-form's interactive submit that defeats prior agents), hard 1/10 PASS in 294s (hard-recoverable; the 500→banner→re-submit path is exactly where the judge mechanism shines). Both ≥3/20 easy and ≥1/10 hard thresholds met. 9 hard failures driven by the CSS-selector substrate's reach (shadow DOM, canvas coords, cross-iframe drag, sustained scroll past 200 rows, binary PDF) — limits of the action layer, not of the speculative-rollback loop; README's 'Live results' section documents this failure analysis even though the threshold made it optional. 280 TS tests + 7 Python tests passing (27 new in speculative_rollback_agent.test.ts). Trajectories generated at runs/speculative-rollback/{easy-*,hard-*}/0/trajectory.jsonl.gz (gitignored per prior precedent).

userStories.title: Mine failure traces and propose >=3 new approach hypotheses

Approaches must NOT duplicate existing agents' approach_keywords

userStories.title: Build mixture meta-agent from top performers
userStories.description: As Ralph, I will build a meta-agent that routes each incoming task to the most appropriate of the top-3 agents from the latest tournament, based on lightweight task features. This tests whether per-task routing beats any single agent.
acceptanceCriteria: agents/meta-mixture/ implements a routing agent that selects among the top-3 agents per task based on cheap features (URL host, goal length, presence of keywords, etc.)
Router decision policy is documented in README.md and is itself trained or tuned ONLY on the easy slice
Meta-agent runs the full hard slice without leakage of hard-slice signal into the router
Tournament re-runs with the meta-agent included; if meta-agent beats every single-mechanism agent on hard, declare it the new champion in docs/champion-<date>.md
If meta-agent does NOT beat the single-mechanism champion, README.md analyses why
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Final post-mortem and next-paradigm proposals
userStories.description: As a stakeholder, I want a written post-mortem covering the full project: what mechanisms won, what surprised us, what failed, and what to try next.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/results.md contains: champion summary, full leaderboard table, ablation findings, failure taxonomy, mechanism-to-success-pct correlation, and >=3 falsifiable next-paradigm hypotheses
Document references concrete trajectory paths and agent README files for every claim
A short 'lessons for the next research umbrella' section identifies which harness pieces should be kept, replaced, or extended
Document is reproducible: every number in it is regenerable from runs/ via `make report`
